title: Campylospermum perseifolium
intro: Campylospermum perseifolium, also known as the West African bitter leaf, is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Campylospermum perseifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tea, capsules, and tinctures, which are sold as herbal supplements to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Campylospermum perseifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It belongs to the genus Campylospermum. The plant is native to tropical Africa. Campylospermum perseifolium is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Further classification places it in the kingdom Plantae.
